```{r echo = F, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
       fill = unique(bbplot$color),
       cex = 1,
       bty = 'n',
       pt.cex = .5,
       xpd = T)

lines(x=c(2,8),
      y=c(60,60))

text(x = 5,
     y = 61,
     labels = "*")

lines(x=c(5,8),
      y=c(57,57))

text(x = 6.5,
     y = 58,
     labels = "*")



```


```{r echo = F, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}

bbplot = bb4

bbplot$color = "grey"
bbplot$color[grep("SaxA", bbplot$Genotype)] = "firebrick"
bbplot$color[grep("gbb", bbplot$Genotype)] = "steelblue"

bbplot$Genotype = factor(bbplot$Genotype, levels = c("WTcr/LoxP",
                                                     "OK371>gbb, WT",
                                                     "OK371>SaxA, WT",
                                                     "G85Rcr/hr",
                                                     "OK371>gbb, G85R",
                                                     "OK371>SaxA, G85R"))

par(mar=c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 6.3))

boxplot(bbplot$Boutons~bbplot$Genotype,
        at = c(1,2,3, 5,6,7),
        xaxt = 'none',
        las = 2,
        xlab = "",
        col = hex(c("grey", "steelblue", "firebrick"), .25),
        ylab = 'Total Boutons',
        main = "Segment 4 Boutons",
        ylim = c(0,60))


beeswarm(bbplot$Boutons~bbplot$Genotype,
         at = c(1,2,3, 5,6,7),
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1.0,
       spacing = 1.0,
       method = "swarm",
       bg = c("grey", "steelblue", "firebrick"),
       add = T)


axis(side = 1,
     at = c(2, 6),
     labels = c("WT(cr/hr)", "G85R(cr/hr)"))

legend('topright',
       inset=c(-0.225,0),
       legend = c('Control', 'gbb', "SaxA"), 
       fill = c("grey", "steelblue", "firebrick"),
       cex = 1,
       bty = 'n',
       pt.cex = .5,
       xpd = T)

lines(x=c(5,7),
      y=c(60,60))

lines(x=c(6,7),
      y=c(57,57))

text(x = 6,
     y = 61,
     labels = "*")

text(x = 6.5,
     y = 58,
     labels = "*")

abline(v = 4, lty = 2)

```


```{r echo = F, warning=F}

print("Generated a linear model using all of the data collected on segment A4 to detect main effects of the Rescue and Model")
A4 <- subset(bb, Segment=="A4")

fit <- lm(Boutons ~ Model * Rescue, data=A4)

anova(fit)

print("The model did not have a significant main effect alone but the rescue did and the interaction effect of the model and rescue was also significant. This indicates that gbb and SaxA are significantly altering bouton number and this effect is different between the genotypes.")

emmeans(fit, pairwise ~ Model * Rescue,
        adjust="tukey")

print("Individual comparisons found that the effects of SaxA in G85R significantly altered bouton counts from G85R in the control and gbb backgrounds")

```


```{r echo = F, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
##Barron's Boutons

bb5 = bb[bb$Segment == "A5", c('Genotype', "Boutons")]

bbplot = bb5

bbplot$color = "WT(cr)"
bbplot$color[grep("G85R", bbplot$Genotype)] = "G85R(cr)"
bbplot$color = std.colors[bbplot$color]

bbplot$Genotype = factor(bbplot$Genotype, levels = c("WTcr/LoxP", "G85Rcr/hr",
                                                     "OK371>gbb, WT",
                                                     "OK371>gbb, G85R",
                                                     "OK371>SaxA, WT",
                                                     "OK371>SaxA, G85R"))

par(mar=c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 6.3))

boxplot(bbplot$Boutons~bbplot$Genotype,
        at = c(1,2, 4,5, 7,8),
        xaxt = 'none',
        las = 2,
        xlab = "Genotype",
        col = hex(unique(bbplot$color), .25),
        ylab = 'Total Boutons',
        main = "Segment 5 Boutons",
        ylim = c(0,60))


beeswarm(bbplot$Boutons~bbplot$Genotype,
         at = c(1,2, 4,5, 7,8),
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1.0,
       spacing = 1.0,
       method = "swarm",
       bg = unique(bbplot$color),
       add = T)


axis(side = 1,
     at = c(1.5, 4.5, 7.5),
     labels = c("Control", "OK371>gbb", "OK371>SaxA"))

legend('topright',
       inset=c(-0.225,0),
       legend = c('WT(cr/hr)', 'G85R(cr/hr)'), 
       fill = unique(bbplot$color),
       cex = 1,
       bty = 'n',
       pt.cex = .5,
       xpd = T)

lines(x=c(1,2),
      y=c(60,60))

text(x = 1.5,
     y = 61,
     labels = "*")

lines(x=c(1,4),
      y=c(57,57))

text(x = 2.5,
     y = 58,
     labels = "*")

lines(x=c(2,8),
      y=c(54,54))

text(x = 5,
     y = 55,
     labels = "*")



```


```{r echo = F, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}

bbplot = bb5

bbplot$color = "grey"
bbplot$color[grep("SaxA", bbplot$Genotype)] = "firebrick"
bbplot$color[grep("gbb", bbplot$Genotype)] = "steelblue"

bbplot$Genotype = factor(bbplot$Genotype, levels = c("WTcr/LoxP",
                                                     "OK371>gbb, WT",
                                                     "OK371>SaxA, WT",
                                                     "G85Rcr/hr",
                                                     "OK371>gbb, G85R",
                                                     "OK371>SaxA, G85R"))

par(mar=c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 6.3))

boxplot(bbplot$Boutons~bbplot$Genotype,
        at = c(1,2,3, 5,6,7),
        xaxt = 'none',
        las = 2,
        xlab = "",
        col = hex(c("grey", "steelblue", "firebrick"), .25),
        ylab = 'Total Boutons',
        main = "Segment 5 Boutons",
        ylim = c(0,60))


beeswarm(bbplot$Boutons~bbplot$Genotype,
         at = c(1,2,3, 5,6,7),
       pch = 21,
       cex = 1.0,
       spacing = 1.0,
       method = "swarm",
       bg = c("grey", "steelblue", "firebrick"),
       add = T)


axis(side = 1,
     at = c(2, 6),
     labels = c("WT(cr/hr)", "G85R(cr/hr)"))

legend('topright',
       inset=c(-0.225,0),
       legend = c('Control', 'gbb', "SaxA"), 
       fill = c("grey", "steelblue", "firebrick"),
       cex = 1,
       bty = 'n',
       pt.cex = .5,
       xpd = T)

lines(x=c(1,5),
      y=c(60,60))

lines(x=c(1,2),
      y=c(57,57))

lines(x=c(5,7),
      y=c(57,57))

text(x = 2.5,
     y = 61,
     labels = "*")

text(x = 1.5,
     y = 58,
     labels = "*")

text(x = 6,
     y = 58,
     labels = "*")

abline(v = 4, lty = 2)

```


```{r echo = F, warning=F}

print("Generated a linear model using all of the data collected on segment A4 to detect main effects of the Rescue and Model")
A5 <- subset(bb, Segment=="A5")

fit <- lm(Boutons ~ Model * Rescue, data=A5)

anova(fit)

print("Neither the model or rescue had a significant main effect alone but the interaction effect of the model and rescue together was significant. This indicates that gbb and SaxA are significantly altering bouton number differently between the genotypes.")

emmeans(fit, pairwise ~ Model * Rescue,
        adjust="tukey")

print("Individual comparisons found that only WT and G85R have significantly different numbers of boutons at an adj. p of .05. Using a threshold of .1, the comparisons G85R ~ G85R-SaxA and WT ~ WT-gbb were found to have  significantly altered bouton counts")

```


##Aleah embryo data

```{r echo = F, fig.width=5, fig.height=5}

##Embryo survival data
embryo = read.csv("/Users/johncsantiago/Documents/GitHub/WhartonLab/RussoPaper/AleahEmbryoSurvivalData.csv")

embryo$Percent = 100*(embryo$Dead/embryo$Total)
plot.embryo = data.frame(WT = embryo$Percent[embryo$Genotype == "Wtcr/loxP"],
                         G85R = embryo$Percent[embryo$Genotype == "G85Rcr/hr"])


embryo.labels = c("WTcr/loxP", "G85Rcr/hr")

embryo.cols = std.colors[c("WT(cr)","G85R(cr)")]

n.embryo = setNames(rep(0, ncol(plot.embryo)), colnames(plot.embryo))
mean.embryo = n.embryo
sd.embryo = n.embryo

i=1
while(i<= ncol(plot.embryo)){
  mean.embryo[i] = mean(na.omit(plot.embryo[,i]))
  sd.embryo[i] = sd(na.omit(plot.embryo[,i]))
  n.embryo[i] = (length(na.omit(plot.embryo[,i])))
  i=i+1
}

stderr.embryo = sd.embryo/(sqrt(n.embryo))

bar.colors = hex(embryo.cols, .75)

border.colors = embryo.cols

tick.x = c(.7 + (c(0:1)*1.2))

par(mar = c(8,4,4,2), lwd = 3)

barplot(height = mean.embryo,
        ylim = c(0, 20),
        col = border.colors,
        las = 2,
        ylab = "% Dead",
        main = "Embryo Survival",
        border = "black",
        xaxt = "n")

axis(side = 1,
     at = tick.x,
     lwd = 3,
     line = 0,
     labels = embryo.labels,
     xpd = T)

axis(side = 2,
     at = c(0,5,10,15,20),
     lwd = 3,
     line = 0,
     labels = F,
     xpd = T)
lines(x = c(0.1, 2.5),
     y = c(0,0),
     lwd = 3,
     xpd = T)

i = 1
while(i <= length(stderr.embryo)){
  lines(x = tick.x[c(i,i)],
        y = c(mean.embryo[i], (mean.embryo[i] + stderr.embryo[i])),
        lwd = 3,
        col = "black")
  
  lines(x = c((tick.x[i] - .25), (tick.x[i] + .25)),
        y = c((mean.embryo[i] + stderr.embryo[i]), (mean.embryo[i] + stderr.embryo[i])),
        lwd = 3,
        col = "black")
  
  i = i +1
}

lines(x = tick.x[1:2],
      y =  c( 15,15),
      lwd = 3)

lines(x = c(1.9,3.1),
      y = c(63, 63),
      lwd = 3)

text(x = 1.3,
     y = 17,
    labels = "*",
    cex = 2,
    xpd = t)





##Stats advice from chatgpt:
##A classic Student’s t-test assumes:
##  Normal distribution (hard to justify with n=4)
##  Similar variances
##  No strong outliers

##Your data violates at least #1 (and maybe #2). With such a small sample, the t-test becomes very unstable — a single value (like that 0) can heavily influence the result.

##Wilcoxon rank-sum test (recommended)
##  Non-parametric → no normality assumption
##  Works well with small n
##  Tests whether one group tends to be larger than the other

##This uses the exact distribution, which is more appropriate than approximations at small n.

wilcox.test(plot.embryo$WT, 
            plot.embryo$G85R, 
            exact = TRUE)

```
